meaning: adj.发音不和谐的,粗腔横调的
examples: And the city is just too cacophonous for meaningful conversation.
As expected, birds raised in soundproof boxes grew up to sing cacophonous songs.
Mouhot spent time in this cacophonous town, where traders sold all manner of goods.
The sound that issues from the vuvuzela is cacophonous -- joyful and infuriating at the same time.
None of this is remotely relevant in today's America, with its religious diversity, cacophonous culture wars and out-of-control political partisanship.
